memory.title: Memory & GC
memory.chart.title: Heap size over time
memory.chart.waiting: Waiting for more GC events to plot a trend...
memory.events.title: Recent GC events
memory.guide.title: What the generations mean
memory.guide.g0: Short-lived objects collected most often. Cheap and frequent; high counts here are normal.
memory.guide.g1: Objects that survived one collection. A middle tier between short-lived and long-lived.
memory.guide.g2: Long-lived objects, including the large object heap. Collections here are rarer and more expensive; frequent gen 2 activity signals real memory pressure.
memory.guide.interval: Interval is the time between GC observer polls, not the GC pause itself. Mono does not expose pause durations, so this is the sampling cadence rather than how long the game stalled.
metrics.title: Custom Metrics
metrics.col.name: Metric
patches.title: Harmony Patch Conflicts
patches.empty.hint: No other mod patches any method RimObs instruments. Nothing is competing for these methods.
patches.intro: Other mods patching the same methods RimObs instruments. This is informational, not an error - Harmony composes multiple patches. Use it to attribute behavior or overhead to a specific mod.
logs.title: Collector Logs
sections.title: Sections
sections.filter.label: Filter by subsystem
sessions.title: Sessions
settings.title: Settings
tip.settings.prometheus: When enabled, the collector serves a Prometheus-format /metrics endpoint built from session aggregates.
settings.close_on_disconnect.hint: Close this tab automatically when the collector stops responding (for example, when RimWorld exits). May be blocked by your browser if this tab was not opened by a script.
tip.overview.tps: Ticks per second. RimWorld's simulation clock. Normal speed targets 60 TPS; below 30 indicates the game is struggling to keep up.
tip.overview.fps: Frames rendered per second. Independent of TPS - the game can render smoothly even when the simulation lags.
tip.overview.batches: Total MessagePack telemetry batches received from the library over UDP since the session started.
tip.overview.samples: Total individual samples (timing measurements, metric points, GC events) carried inside those batches.
tip.overview.sections: Number of distinct instrumented code regions currently reporting timings.
tip.overview.gc: Garbage collection events observed since the session started. High counts at gen 2 indicate real memory pressure.
tip.overview.allocations: Estimated allocation events attributed to instrumented sections. Heuristic based on GC.GetTotalMemory deltas.
tip.overview.bytes: Wire bytes received over UDP, before MessagePack decoding.
tip.hotspots.section: Code region identifier. Auto-prefixed with the mod's packageId at registration.
tip.hotspots.total: Cumulative time spent inside this section across all samples in the rolling window.
tip.hotspots.mean: Average time per sample. Useful when comparing sections by cost-per-call.
tip.hotspots.p50: Median latency. Half of samples are faster than this.
tip.hotspots.p95: 95th percentile latency. Reflects typical tail behavior - this is what 5% of calls feel like.
tip.hotspots.p99: 99th percentile latency. The worst 1% of samples. Spikes here indicate occasional stalls users notice.
tip.hotspots.samples: How many times this section was timed in the rolling window.
tip.memory.currentHeap: Heap size at the last GC observation. Mono managed heap only, not the full process RSS.
tip.memory.peakHeap: Largest heap size seen during the session. Resets when the session restarts.
tip.memory.peakRate: Highest sustained allocation rate observed, in bytes per minute.
tip.memory.collections: Collections at generation 0 / 1 / 2. Gen 0 should dominate; high gen 2 counts mean long-lived garbage or large objects.
tip.memory.col.gen: GC generation. Gen 0 is short-lived, gen 1 survived one collection, gen 2 includes the large object heap.
tip.memory.col.pause: Pause classification reported by the GC observer.
tip.memory.col.before: Heap size just before this collection ran.
tip.memory.col.after: Heap size right after the collection completed.
tip.memory.col.interval: Time since the previous GC observer poll. Not the actual GC pause - Mono does not expose pause durations.
tip.memory.col.rate: Bytes allocated per minute leading up to this collection.
tip.memory.col.tick: RimWorld tick counter when this event was observed.
tip.metrics.kind: Metric type. Counter = monotonically increasing, gauge = current value, histogram = distribution of samples.
tip.metrics.unit: Unit of measurement declared at registration. Free-form string (e.g., ms, bytes, items).
tip.metrics.labels: Label sets currently observed for this metric. Cardinality is capped per section to prevent runaway memory.
tip.metrics.value: Most recent value reported for this label combination.
tip.metrics.samples: How many points have been recorded against this label combination so far.
tip.patches.owner: The packageId of the mod that applied the patch.
tip.patches.type: Harmony patch kind: prefix runs before, postfix runs after, transpiler rewrites IL, finalizer runs in a try/catch, reverse calls original.
tip.patches.priority: Harmony's priority ordering. Higher priority runs earlier within its patch kind.
tip.patches.method: Fully-qualified name of the patch method itself, useful for tracing back to the owning mod's code.
tip.sessions.id: Unique session identifier assigned by the collector when the library first connected.
tip.sessions.started: Wall-clock time the library handshake completed.
tip.sessions.library: Version of RimObs.Library running inside RimWorld for this session.
tip.sessions.game: RimWorld version string captured at session start, if reported.
tip.sessions.sections: Distinct instrumented sections registered during this session.
tip.sessions.metrics: Distinct custom metrics registered during this session.
tip.sessions.batches: MessagePack telemetry batches received from this session.
tip.sessions.samples: Individual timing/metric samples carried inside those batches.
tip.sessions.sectionTime: Sum of all section durations across the session.
tip.sessions.gc: GC events observed during this session.
tip.sessions.allocations: Attributed allocations observed during this session.
tip.sessions.bytes: Wire bytes received from this session.
tip.sessions.lastBatch: How long ago the most recent batch arrived from this session.
tip.settings.version: Collector binary version, semver. Bumped automatically on release via semantic-release.
tip.settings.schema: Wire schema version. Increments when the MessagePack batch layout changes incompatibly.
tip.settings.language: Active dashboard language. Override with the ?lang= query parameter.
tip.instrumentation.active: Library has applied the patch and is collecting samples.
tip.instrumentation.pending: Patch was requested but the library has not confirmed it yet.
tip.instrumentation.stale: Library reported the patch was removed or the target method no longer exists.
tip.gen.g0: Generation 0 - short-lived objects. Most allocations land here and are collected quickly.
tip.gen.g1: Generation 1 - objects that survived one collection. A middle tier.
tip.gen.g2: Generation 2 - long-lived objects and the large object heap. Expensive to collect.
bundle.title: Diagnostic Bundles
bundle.subtitle: Package a session for sharing, or inspect a bundle someone sent you.
bundle.export.title: Export a bundle
bundle.export.noSession: No active session. Launch RimWorld with the framework mod to start one.
bundle.export.contentsHint: Manifest, batches, sections, metrics and the report are always included. Tick extras below.
bundle.export.overCapWarning: Over the 25 MB soft cap. Large bundles are slow to share.
bundle.export.forceHint: Override the 25 MB soft cap. Large bundles are slow to share.
bundle.import.title: Inspect a bundle
bundle.import.error: Couldn't read that bundle. Make sure it's a .rimobs.zip exported by RimObs.
comparison.head: Comparison target
comparison.disclaimer: Deltas indicate correlation, not causation. Flagged items are likely regression candidates, not confirmed causes.
comparison.loadOrderNote: Owners are derived from section name prefixes, not the game load order.
comparison.col.head: Head
